title: Emerging Technologies and Future Networking
description: Practice understanding Wi-Fi 7, 5G private networks, intent-based networking, SASE/SSE, post-quantum cryptography, AIOps, and network programmability trends.
starterCode:
# EMERGING TECHNOLOGIES EXERCISE

## Part 1: Wi-Fi 7 (802.11be)

1. Wi-Fi 7 is defined by IEEE standard 802.11______.
2. MLO (Multi-Link _______) allows simultaneous transmission across multiple frequency bands.
3. Wi-Fi 7 supports channel widths up to _______ MHz — double the maximum of Wi-Fi 6.
4. 4096-QAM (4K-QAM) encodes _______ bits per symbol, a 20% improvement over 1024-QAM.
5. Wi-Fi 7 operates across three bands: 2.4 GHz, 5 GHz, and _______ GHz.
6. An MLD (Multi-Link _______) presents a single MAC address while operating across multiple bands.
7. MLO provides sub-_______ failover when one band experiences congestion or interference.

## Part 2: 5G Private Networks

8. In the US, CBRS uses Band _______ (3550-3700 MHz) for private 5G deployments.
9. CBRS has three tiers: Incumbent, PAL, and _______ (General Authorized Access — free tier).
10. A _______ manages CBRS spectrum sharing and interference coordination.
11. 5G URLLC provides ultra-reliable low-latency communication with sub-_______ latency.
12. Private 5G supports up to _______ million devices per square kilometer — far exceeding Wi-Fi density.
13. A 5G _______ (next-generation Node B) is the radio base station in a 5G network.
14. Private 5G is most suited for _______ environments like factories, warehouses, and hospitals.

## Part 3: Intent-Based Networking (IBN)

15. IBN shifts focus from device _______ to desired business outcomes.
16. The four layers of IBN are: Intent, Translation, Activation, and _______.
17. Cisco DNA Center uses SD-Access with _______ overlay for network segmentation.
18. The assurance layer uses AI/ML and _______ to detect anomalies and verify intent compliance.
19. LISP (Locator/ID _______ Protocol) maps endpoint identity to network location in SD-Access.
20. ISE (Identity _______ Engine) defines group-based security policies in Cisco's IBN.
21. IBN automatically _______ configuration drift back to the intended state.

## Part 4: SASE and SSE

22. SASE stands for Secure Access Service _______.
23. SSE is SASE minus the _______-WAN networking component.
24. ZTNA (Zero Trust Network _______) replaces traditional VPN with per-application access.
25. CASB (Cloud Access _______ Broker) provides visibility and control over SaaS application usage.
26. SWG (Secure _______ Gateway) inspects and filters web traffic at the cloud edge.
27. FWaaS stands for Firewall as a _______.
28. SASE delivers security from global PoPs within <_______ ms of 95% of users.

## Part 5: Post-Quantum Cryptography

29. Shor's _______ running on quantum computers can break RSA, ECDSA, and DH.
30. NIST FIPS _______ standardizes ML-KEM (CRYSTALS-Kyber) for key encapsulation.
31. NIST FIPS 204 standardizes ML-DSA (CRYSTALS-_______) for digital signatures.
32. The "Harvest Now, Decrypt _______" threat means adversaries capture encrypted data for future quantum decryption.
33. NSA CNSA 2.0 requires post-quantum migration by the year _______.
34. ML-DSA signatures are approximately _______ KB — much larger than 72-byte ECDSA signatures.
35. Hybrid key exchange combines classical (X25519) with post-quantum (_______) in TLS 1.3.

## Part 6: Network Programmability

36. NETCONF uses _______ encoding over SSH for programmatic device configuration (RFC 6241).
37. RESTCONF uses JSON or XML over _______ for RESTful device configuration (RFC 8040).
38. _______ data models define the structure of network device configurations.
39. gRPC/gNMI enables streaming _______ for real-time network state monitoring.
40. Ansible automates network configuration using _______ (playbook format) with no agent required.
41. _______ is used for infrastructure provisioning, defining infrastructure as code with declarative syntax.
42. AIOps uses machine _______ to establish baselines, detect anomalies, and predict failures.
solution:
# SOLUTION

## Part 1: Wi-Fi 7 (802.11be)

1. Wi-Fi 7 is defined by IEEE standard 802.11**be**.
2. MLO (Multi-Link **Operation**) allows simultaneous transmission across multiple frequency bands.
3. Wi-Fi 7 supports channel widths up to **320** MHz — double the maximum of Wi-Fi 6.
4. 4096-QAM (4K-QAM) encodes **12** bits per symbol, a 20% improvement over 1024-QAM.
5. Wi-Fi 7 operates across three bands: 2.4 GHz, 5 GHz, and **6** GHz.
6. An MLD (Multi-Link **Device**) presents a single MAC address while operating across multiple bands.
7. MLO provides sub-**millisecond** failover when one band experiences congestion or interference.

## Part 2: 5G Private Networks

8. In the US, CBRS uses Band **48** (3550-3700 MHz) for private 5G deployments.
9. CBRS has three tiers: Incumbent, PAL, and **GAA** (General Authorized Access — free tier).
10. A **SAS** (Spectrum Access System) manages CBRS spectrum sharing and interference coordination.
11. 5G URLLC provides ultra-reliable low-latency communication with sub-**1ms** latency.
12. Private 5G supports up to **1** million devices per square kilometer — far exceeding Wi-Fi density.
13. A 5G **gNB** (next-generation Node B) is the radio base station in a 5G network.
14. Private 5G is most suited for **industrial** environments like factories, warehouses, and hospitals.

## Part 3: Intent-Based Networking (IBN)

15. IBN shifts focus from device **configuration** to desired business outcomes.
16. The four layers of IBN are: Intent, Translation, Activation, and **Assurance**.
17. Cisco DNA Center uses SD-Access with **VXLAN** overlay for network segmentation.
18. The assurance layer uses AI/ML and **telemetry** to detect anomalies and verify intent compliance.
19. LISP (Locator/ID **Separation** Protocol) maps endpoint identity to network location in SD-Access.
20. ISE (Identity **Services** Engine) defines group-based security policies in Cisco's IBN.
21. IBN automatically **remediates** configuration drift back to the intended state.

## Part 4: SASE and SSE

22. SASE stands for Secure Access Service **Edge**.
23. SSE is SASE minus the **SD**-WAN networking component.
24. ZTNA (Zero Trust Network **Access**) replaces traditional VPN with per-application access.
25. CASB (Cloud Access **Security** Broker) provides visibility and control over SaaS application usage.
26. SWG (Secure **Web** Gateway) inspects and filters web traffic at the cloud edge.
27. FWaaS stands for Firewall as a **Service**.
28. SASE delivers security from global PoPs within <**20** ms of 95% of users.

## Part 5: Post-Quantum Cryptography

29. Shor's **algorithm** running on quantum computers can break RSA, ECDSA, and DH.
30. NIST FIPS **203** standardizes ML-KEM (CRYSTALS-Kyber) for key encapsulation.
31. NIST FIPS 204 standardizes ML-DSA (CRYSTALS-**Dilithium**) for digital signatures.
32. The "Harvest Now, Decrypt **Later**" threat means adversaries capture encrypted data for future quantum decryption.
33. NSA CNSA 2.0 requires post-quantum migration by the year **2033**.
34. ML-DSA signatures are approximately **2.4** KB — much larger than 72-byte ECDSA signatures.
35. Hybrid key exchange combines classical (X25519) with post-quantum (**Kyber768**) in TLS 1.3.

## Part 6: Network Programmability

36. NETCONF uses **XML** encoding over SSH for programmatic device configuration (RFC 6241).
37. RESTCONF uses JSON or XML over **HTTPS** for RESTful device configuration (RFC 8040).
38. **YANG** data models define the structure of network device configurations.
39. gRPC/gNMI enables streaming **telemetry** for real-time network state monitoring.
40. Ansible automates network configuration using **YAML** (playbook format) with no agent required.
41. **Terraform** is used for infrastructure provisioning, defining infrastructure as code with declarative syntax.
42. AIOps uses machine **learning** to establish baselines, detect anomalies, and predict failures.
hints: Wi-Fi 7 = 802.11be; MLO = Multi-Link Operation; maximum channel width doubles to 320 MHz.
4096-QAM encodes 12 bits per symbol (log2(4096) = 12); Wi-Fi 7 tri-band includes 6 GHz.
CBRS uses Band 48 (3550-3700 MHz); GAA (General Authorized Access) is the free tier.
SAS (Spectrum Access System) coordinates CBRS; 5G gNB is the next-generation base station.
IBN layers: Intent → Translation → Activation → Assurance; uses VXLAN overlay in SD-Access.
LISP = Locator/ID Separation Protocol; ISE = Identity Services Engine.
SASE = Secure Access Service Edge; SSE = SASE minus SD-WAN; ZTNA = Zero Trust Network Access.
CASB = Cloud Access Security Broker; SWG = Secure Web Gateway; FWaaS = Firewall as a Service.
FIPS 203 = ML-KEM (Kyber); FIPS 204 = ML-DSA (Dilithium); CNSA 2.0 deadline = 2033.
NETCONF uses XML/SSH; RESTCONF uses JSON/HTTPS; YANG defines config models; Terraform = IaC.
